title: Biophytum molle
intro: Biophytum molle, also known as Indian bread or Saptaparna, is a type of succulent plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, commonly found in the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. Biophytum molle contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being made into powders and capsules, to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Biophytum molle is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Biophytum, which consists of several species of herbaceous plants. The genus Biophytum is part of the order Oxalidales and the class Magnoliopsida. The kingdom to which Biophytum molle belongs is Plantae. It is a perennial herb native to tropical regions.
